params.name: Fenian Cycle
params.description:
On May 26, 983 AD, the stories were compiled into a book by the Irish people.
The Fenian Cycle is a collection of famous Gaelic stories and ballads in ancient Ireland and one of the four ancient Irish folk legends. The book tells of the great hero Fionn and his Fianna warriors' adventurous legends of bravery and sacrifice. Under the leadership of the hero Fionn, they defeated demons and giants, resisted foreign invasions, defended local order and peace, and made numerous heroic feats. The sto …
